problem_addressed: Existing semi-supervised learning methods do not incorporate uncertainty quantification for pseudo-labels or unlabeled samples, leading to issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations.
core_claim: Complementing SSL with an uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty improves performance by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.
key_contributions: Proposing a novel uncertainty-aware objective incorporating both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty for SSL
Implementing the approach by complementing contemporary SSL work with the uncertainty framework
Achieving state-of-the-art performance on standard SSL benchmarks including CIFAR-100 and Mini-ImageNet
novelty: This is the first work to estimate and align uncertainty distributions of labeled and unlabeled data in SSL, whereas previous efforts focused solely on consistency regularization without uncertainty quantification.
method_summary: The approach proposes an unsupervised uncertainty-aware objective that quantifies aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty to align distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains, complementing existing SSL techniques without requiring computationally expensive graph formation or similarity computations.
method_components: Consistency regularization between weakly and strongly augmented samples
Uncertainty quantification using both aleatoric and epistemic measures
key_innovation: Incorporating uncertainty quantification into the SSL training process to handle noisy pseudo-labels and out-of-distribution samples by aligning uncertainty distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains.
assumptions: Unlabeled data has similar distribution to labeled data
Uncertainty quantification can effectively identify problematic samples
Strong augmentations may introduce out-of-distribution samples
hyperparameters: Confidence threshold τc=0.95; αUE=1, λ=0.1; αUA=75 (CIFAR-10/SVHN), 150 (CIFAR-100), 300 (Mini-ImageNet); SGD optimizer (lr=0.01/0.03, weight decay=5e-4) for CIFAR-10/SVHN/CIFAR-100; AdamW (lr=0.002, weight decay=2e-2) for Mini-ImageNet; Wide-ResNet-28-2 (CIFAR-10/SVHN), WRN-28-8 (CIFAR-100), ResNet-18 (Mini-ImageNet)
improvement_over_baseline: 0.08% over SimPLE on CIFAR-10 (1000 labels); 0.07% over SimPLE on CIFAR-100 (4000 labels)
stated_limitations: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo labels or unlabeled samples
Issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations
unstated_weaknesses: Lack of specific details on uncertainty quantification methods used
No discussion of computational complexity or scalability
No evaluation on medical imaging datasets
No comparison with other uncertainty-aware SSL methods
threats_to_validity: Assumption of similar distributions between labeled and unlabeled domains may not hold in medical contexts
Potential challenges with class imbalance and small sample sizes in medical imaging
Limited evaluation across different types and levels of label noise
open_questions: How does the proposed method perform specifically on medical imaging datasets?
What is the computational overhead of the uncertainty quantification approach?
How does the method handle different types of label noise in medical contexts?
Can the uncertainty-aware objective be adapted for domain-specific medical challenges?
future_work: Application to specific medical imaging domains
Development of domain-specific uncertainty quantification techniques
Integration with medical imaging preprocessing pipelines
gaps_this_enables: Development of uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection methods for medical imaging
More robust SSL approaches that account for label noise in medical datasets
Combination of uncertainty quantification with domain adaptation for medical images
relevance_to_topic: This paper directly addresses uncertainty quantification in SSL which is fundamental to developing uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection methods for medical image classification under label noise.
limitations_stated: Existing SSL methods do not incorporate uncertainty of pseudo labels or unlabeled samples
Issues with noisy labels and out-of-distribution samples due to strong augmentations
title: Complementing Semi-Supervised Learning with Uncertainty Quantification
